acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.CfnA: ASN1 subject for the certificate authority.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Comm.Description: Fully qualified domain name (FQDN) associated with the certificate subject.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Coun.Description: Two-digit code that specifies the country in which the certificate subject located.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Cust.Description: Contains a sequence of one or more X.500 relative distinguished names (RDNs), each of which consists of an object identifier (OID) and a value. For more information, see NIST’s definition of [Object Identifier (OID)](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/term/Object_Identifier).  Custom attributes cannot be used in combination with standard attributes.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Dist.Description: Disambiguating information for the certificate subject.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Gene.Description: Typically a qualifier appended to the name of an individual. Examples include Jr. for junior, Sr. for senior, and III for third.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Init.Description: Concatenation that typically contains the first letter of the GivenName, the first letter of the middle name if one exists, and the first letter of the SurName.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Loca.Description: The locality (such as a city or town) in which the certificate subject is located.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Orga.Description: Legal name of the organization with which the certificate subject is affiliated.
A subdivision or unit of the organization (such as sales or finance) with which the certificate subject is affiliated.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Pseu.Description: Typically a shortened version of a longer GivenName. For example, Jonathan is often shortened to John. Elizabeth is often shortened to Beth, Liz, or Eliza.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Stat.Description: State in which the subject of the certificate is located.

acmpca.certificateauthority.subject.Titl.Description: A personal title such as Mr.
